title: pleopeltis adfinis
intro: Pleopeltis adfinis, also known as the Resurrection fern, is a type of epiphytic fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is native to the southeastern United States and is known for its unique ability to revive itself after drying out by curling up its fronds and appearing dead. Medicinally, Pleopeltis adfinis has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and skin conditions, due to its high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The plant can be prepared as a tincture or tea, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system.
